proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +40.7pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +40.7pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +40.7pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 1), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is only 11/127 (8.7%) and the only flagged concentration (54.33%) is on Choppy, below the 80% trigger, so promotion does not compound risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-27) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.8pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Regime is shifting (Bull day 1, prior 7d dominated by Choppy with only 1 Bear day) — Bear-gated cohort may remain dormant for extended periods, reducing realized utility of promotion
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +30.8pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +30.8pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear and current regime just shifted to Bull (day 1, shifting) so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is only 11/127 (8.7%) and regime concentration (54.33%) is below the 80% trigger, so promotion does not compound concentration risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 06-27) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (96.1%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share falls below 0.85 via natural Day-6 exits; prioritize any DOWN_TRI or BULL_PROXY-offsetting setups if they trigger. Avoid further adds in Bank sector specifically (largest sector bucket at 29 positions).
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip or broad-market down day would unwind 124 long-directional positions simultaneously with no validated cohort edge to justify holding through drawdown; Bank-sector cluster (29 positions) amplifies single-sector tail risk if financials lead the selloff.

evidence: Long-directional book: UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 124/127 = 97.6%; only 3 DOWN_TRI hedges
Matched cohort Choppy×UP_TRI is Candidate tier (n=256) — unvalidated edge
Top cell UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank at 14.17% (18 positions) — within cell tolerance but reinforces Bank+long clustering
Regime split Choppy 54.3% / Bull 37.0% / Bear 8.7% — mixed regime context with no validated edge backing the long monoculture
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share falls below 0.85 via natural Day-6 exits; prioritize any DOWN_TRI or BULL_PROXY-offsetting setups if they trigger. Avoid further adds in Bank sector specifically (largest sector bucket at 29 positions).
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip or broad-market down day would unwind 124 long-directional positions simultaneously with no validated cohort edge to justify holding through drawdown; Bank-sector cluster (29 positions) amplifies single-sector tail risk if financials lead the selloff.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates the signal mix at 122/127 = 96.1% (well above the 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. The matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=256) — unvalidated edge. While regime concentration (Choppy 54.3%) and the top cell (UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank at 14.17%) are within tolerance, the signal-type concentration on an unvalidated cohort is the actionable risk.
